summary: iter294: Verified UI-manageable owner recovery emails feature. New endpoints/behavior: GET /api/system/email-settings now returns 'recovery_emails' array; PUT accepts recovery_emails list with validation (must have @, lowercased, deduped, 1..5 items). PUT without recovery_emails preserves the existing DB value. get_owner_recovery_emails() helper reads DB dynamically (used by notify_owner_multichannel). 11/11 new iter294 tests pass; 41/41 previous regression tests still pass (shift/expense/notify_multichannel/iter288/iter293). Total: 52/52.

minor.issue: recovery_emails=[] (explicit empty list) returns 400 with the 'at least one valid address' message — behavior matches spec, but the Arabic message could be more explicit ('cannot be empty') vs current one which says 'must contain at least one valid'. Not a bug; UI copy suggestion only.

verification_details.get_email_settings: Returns recovery_emails array (currently ['owner@maestroegp.com','moataz0111@gmail.com','test-backup@example.com']); requires auth (401 without token).
verification_details.put_saves_and_lowercases: PUT with ['TEST_A@Example.com','test_b@example.com'] → 200 {success:true, recovery_emails:['test_a@example.com','test_b@example.com']}. DB confirmed via mongosh. Subsequent GET reflects immediately.
verification_details.put_validation_invalid: PUT ['invalid-email'] → HTTP 400 with Arabic error containing 'بريد الاسترداد' + 'صالح'.
verification_details.put_validation_max5: PUT with 6 emails → HTTP 400 with 'بحد أقصى 5'. Exactly 5 → 200 OK.
verification_details.put_preserves_on_omission: PUT without recovery_emails field (only SMTP fields) does NOT clear DB value — subsequent GET still returns the previously-saved list.
verification_details.helper_reads_db_dynamic: asyncio.run(get_owner_recovery_emails()) after PUT returns the newly-saved list (not the .env fallback), confirming notify_owner_multichannel will send to the UI-managed list.
verification_details.regression: 41/41 pass across test_ahmed_shift_close_regression, test_lazy_shift_and_daily_isolation, test_expense_shift_isolation, test_iter288_shift_id_mandatory, test_notify_multichannel_iter292, test_multichannel_otp_iter293.

critical_code_review_comments: Validation logic in super_admin_routes.py PUT lines 338-354 is clean: string input accepted (comma/newline split), list validated, lowercased, deduped, len bounds enforced. Good.
get_owner_recovery_emails() correctly falls back to _ENV_OWNER_RECOVERY_EMAILS when DB has empty/missing list — safe boot behavior.
notify_owner_multichannel now calls `await get_owner_recovery_emails()` on each notification — this means UI updates take effect immediately for the NEXT notification, no restart needed. Excellent.
PUT response includes recovery_emails in the success payload — good UX (UI can bind directly without re-GET).

Minor: The empty-list branch (`recovery_emails: []`) currently produces the same 'need at least one valid' error as invalid entries. Both are correct behaviors but the UI may want to differentiate 'you cleared the field' vs 'the address is malformed'. Non-blocking.
server.py is still 17,169 lines — same pre-existing observation as iter292/293. Not blocking.

get_owner_recovery_emails() writes to a module-level global OWNER_RECOVERY_EMAILS every call. Given sync helpers may read this cache, there's a minor thread-safety concern under high concurrency, but effectively harmless (idempotent list assignment).
test_credentials: Used: owner@maestroegp.com / owner123 / secret_key=271018 (super admin). 2FA currently disabled in DB — login succeeds directly.
seed_data_creation: Tests temporarily overwrite email_config.recovery_emails; fixture snapshots original value at module start and restores at teardown. Post-run verified via mongosh: original list ['owner@maestroegp.com','moataz0111@gmail.com','test-backup@example.com'] intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter294 verified full CRUD + validation for the new recovery_emails feature in email_config. UI (SuperAdmin.js) can now manage owner recovery emails via textarea+badges. Backend correctly persists, lowercases, dedups, and enforces max=5. notify_owner_multichannel dynamically reads DB list via get_owner_recovery_emails() so UI updates take effect on the next notification. SMTP password is still not set in DB — email delivery will still gracefully fail until user enters it (same state as iter293). WhatsApp still not paired (same state).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — feature works as specified. The user's original question 'where do I enter the backup/recovery email' is now answered by the new UI panel at SuperAdmin → Email Settings → Recovery Emails section. All validation branches match the acceptance criteria.
